subject: [PATCH v5 27/36] mm: add NODE_PRIVATE_CAP_USER_NUMA for userland numa controls

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch adds a mechanism to allow userland NUMA controls on private nodes in the Linux kernel. It introduces a new capability, NODE_PRIVATE_CAP_USER_NUMA, which allows userspace to place or migrate memory on private nodes. The patch also updates various functions and interfaces to enforce this capability, including mbind(), set_mempolicy(), move_pages(), and migrate_pages(). This change enables more fine-grained control over NUMA management in userland applications.
